---
name: "Triage Stats Dashboard"
description: >
  Aggregates all triage tracking issues in this fork and generates/updates
  a pinned dashboard issue with statistics and action items.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  issues: read

tools:
  github:
    toolsets: [repos, issues]
  cache-memory:
  bash: ["cat", "grep", "head", "tail", "wc", "jq", "date", "sort", "uniq", "echo"]

safe-outputs:
  create-issue:
    title-prefix: "[triage-dashboard] "
    labels: [dashboard, automation]
    max: 1
    close-older-issues: true
    expires: false
  update-issue:
    target: "*"
    title-prefix: "[triage-dashboard] "
    body:
    max: 1
  add-comment:
    target: "*"
    max: 1
---

# Triage Stats Dashboard Agent

You are a data aggregation agent. Your job is to collect all triage tracking
issues in this repository and produce a comprehensive dashboard.

## Step 1: Find Existing Dashboard

Search this repo for an open issue with title starting with "[triage-dashboard]".
- If found, you will UPDATE it in Step 4a
- If not found, you will CREATE a new one in Step 4b
- Record the dashboard issue number if found

## Step 2: Collect Triage Data

List all issues in this repository labeled "triage":
- For each issue, extract from the body:
  - **Upstream issue number** and link (from the "Upstream:" field)
  - **Classification** (bug-report, feature-request, duplicate, discussion, wrong-usage, unclear)
  - **Whether reproduction was attempted** and succeeded (from "Reproduced:" field)
  - **Whether a fix was suggested** (presence of "Suggested Fix" section)
  - **Status** (COMPLETE or IN_PROGRESS from "Status:" field)
  - **Area component** (from "Area:" field)
  - **Confidence score** (from "Confidence:" field)

Collect this data for ALL triage issues (paginate if necessary).

## Step 3: Compute Statistics

Calculate the following:
- **Total triaged issues** (count of all triage-labeled issues)
- **Breakdown by classification** (count per category)
- **Reproduction success rate** (for bug reports only: reproduced / attempted)
- **Number with suggested fixes**
- **Pending count** (IN_PROGRESS status)
- **Area distribution** (count per area component)
- **Average confidence score**

### Delta Computation
- Load previous run stats from cache-memory (key: `triage-dashboard-stats`)
- Compare current stats against previous to compute deltas ("+N" or "−N")
- Store current stats in cache-memory for the next run

## Step 4a: Update Existing Dashboard

If a dashboard issue was found in Step 1, use `update-issue` to REPLACE its body
with the new dashboard content (see Dashboard Template below).

## Step 4b: Create New Dashboard

If no dashboard issue was found, use `create-issue` to create one:
- **Title:** `ML.NET Issue Triage Dashboard`
  (will be prefixed with "[triage-dashboard] " automatically)
- **Body:** Use the Dashboard Template below

## Step 5: Add Comment with Delta

Post a comment on the dashboard issue summarizing what changed since the last run:
- New issues triaged since last run
- Classification breakdown changes
- Any new reproductions or fix suggestions

## Dashboard Template

Use this template for the dashboard issue body:

```markdown
# 🔍 ML.NET Issue Triage Dashboard

> Last updated: <current date/time>
> Generated by: Triage Stats Dashboard workflow

## Summary Statistics

| Metric | Count | Δ |
|---|---|---|
| Total Investigated | <count> | <delta> |
| Bug Reports | <count> | <delta> |
| Feature Requests | <count> | <delta> |
| Duplicates | <count> | <delta> |
| Questions/Discussion | <count> | <delta> |
| Wrong Usage | <count> | <delta> |
| Unclear | <count> | <delta> |
| **Reproduced** | **<count>** | **<delta>** |
| Repro Failed | <count> | <delta> |
| Fix Suggested | <count> | <delta> |
| Pending (In Progress) | <count> | <delta> |

## Average Confidence Score: <score>

## By Area

| Area | Bugs | Features | Other | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <area> | <count> | <count> | <count> | <count> |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

## Recent Investigations (Last 10)

| Upstream | Classification | Reproduced? | Fix? | Status | Fork Issue |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [#<N>](https://github.com/dotnet/machinelearning/issues/<N>) | <category emoji> <category> | <status> | <yes/no> | <status> | #<fork_issue> |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

## Action Items

### 🐛 Ready for Fix (reproduced + fix suggested)
- List fork issues that are reproduced AND have a suggested fix

### ❓ Needs Upstream Info
- List fork issues classified as "unclear" / "needs-info"

### 📋 Low-Complexity Fixes
- List fork issues with suggested fixes of "Low" complexity

### 🔁 Potential Duplicates to Verify
- List fork issues classified as "duplicate" for manual verification
```

## Category Emoji Mapping

Use these emojis in the dashboard:
- 🐛 bug-report
- ✨ feature-request
- 🔁 duplicate
- 💬 discussion
- ⚠️ wrong-usage
- ❓ unclear

## If No Triage Issues Found

If there are no issues labeled "triage" in this repository, call noop:
```json
{"noop": {"message": "No triage issues found in this repository"}}
```
